title: Theme of Surat Al-Hashr (الحشر) (The Mustering) (59)
excerpt: Surah Al-Hashr classifies those who identify as Muslims into two distinct groups, each aligning with one of the parties mentioned in the previous Surah, Al-Mujadilah (المجادلة): the Party of Allah (حِزۡبَ ٱللَّهِ) and the Party of Satan (حِزۡبُ ٱلشَّيۡطَـٰنِ).

title: Theme of Surat Al-Mujadilah (المجادلة) (The Dispute) (58)
excerpt: The central theme of Surah Al-Mujadilah (المجادلة) revolves around the existence of two distinct groups in this life, and every individual must choose which one to align with. These two groups are the 'Party of Allah' and the 'Party of Satan.'

title: Theme of Surat Al-Hadeed (The Iron) (57)
excerpt: The central theme of Surah Al-Hadeed is the importance of adopting a balanced approach that integrates spiritual devotion, justice, and industrial strength. These three elements are interconnected and essential for fulfilling the divine mission.

title: Quran and Science – Khutbah at RICC
excerpt: A Friday sermon exploring the relationship between the Quran and modern science, delivered at RICC.

title: Theme of Surat Al-Waqiah (الواقعة) (The Event) (56)
excerpt: The main theme of Surat Al-Waqiah (الواقعة) is the unwavering certainty of the Hereafter and the ultimate destiny of individuals, determined by their faith and deeds.

title: Tadabbur of the Quran through the Lens of Key Terms – Part 7
excerpt: Guidance (هداية) and Misguidance (ضلال) — Understanding the Quran's definition of the important term هداية (Hidayah), meaning 'guidance,' is essential to resolving common misunderstandings.

title: Theme of Surat Al-Rahman (الرحمن) (55)
excerpt: The main theme of Surat Al-Rahman is the overwhelming and boundless mercy and blessings of Allah upon His creations. It highlights the numerous favors of Allah, urging humankind and jinn to recognize and be grateful.

title: Tadabbur of the Quran through the Lens of Key Terms – Part 6
excerpt: A continuation of the Tadabbur series discussing approaches outlined in the Quran to deepen our knowledge and understanding of the religion of Islam.

title: Theme of Surat Al-Qamar (القمر) (The Moon) (54)
excerpt: The Surah delivers a powerful and clear statement emphasizing that Allah is serious about His punishment for those who reject His message. Allah provides several historical examples of His divine justice.

title: Tadabbur of the Quran through the Lens of Key Terms – Part 5
excerpt: A continuation of the Tadabbur series discussing the most important source of knowledge about Islam, doctrines that led to corruption of previous religions, and the Quran's refutation.

title: Theme of Surat An-Najm (النجم) (The Star) (53)
excerpt: Surah An-Najm, revealed in Mecca, highlights key aspects of the Mi'raj — the miraculous night journey and ascension of Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) from Jerusalem to the seventh heaven.

title: Tadabbur of the Quran through the Lens of Key Terms – Part 4
excerpt: A continuation discussing the most important source of knowledge about Islam, doctrines that led to corruption of previous religions, and approaches outlined in the Quran.

title: Theme of Surat At-Tur (الطور) (52)
excerpt: The central theme of Surat At-Tur (الطور) highlights the immense blessings that Allah has bestowed upon humanity and that an individual's ultimate destiny depends on acknowledging Allah's blessings.

title: Theme of Surah Al-Dhariyat (الذاريات) (51)
excerpt: The central theme revolves around the relationship between Allah and humankind. Allah provides everything necessary for humans to sustain their lives, while humans are tasked with worshiping Him.

title: Tadabbur of the Quran through the Lens of Key Terms – Part 3
excerpt: Discussing the most important source of knowledge about Islam, doctrines that led to corruption of previous religions, and approaches to deepen our understanding.

title: The Theme of Surat Qaf (ق) (50)
excerpt: Surah Qaf marks the beginning of the final section of the Qur'an, known as Al-Mufassal (المفصّل). This Surah is significant because Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) often recited it during his Friday sermons.

title: Tadabbur of the Quran through the Lens of Key Terms – Part 2
excerpt: Continuing from Part 1: the most important source of knowledge about Islam, doctrines that led to corruption of previous religions, and the prohibition against ordinary people reading divine scripture.

title: The Theme of Surat Al-Hujurat (49)
excerpt: The theme of Surah Al-Hujurat is the Islamic moral code, encapsulating the framework of submitting to Allah's commands, respecting the Prophet (PBUH), and handling information responsibly.

title: Tadabbur of the Quran through the Lens of Key Terms – Part 1
excerpt: The Most Important Source of Knowledge about the Religion of Islam — exploring the primary sources and their proper ranking.

title: Theme of Surat Al-Fath (The Conquest) (48)
excerpt: Surah Al-Fath was revealed in the 6th year after the Hijra, as the Prophet (PBUH) was returning from signing the Treaty of Hudaybiyah (صلح الحديبية), which caused distress among companions.

title: Theme of Surat Mohammad (محمد) (47)
excerpt: The theme highlights the contrast between believers and disbelievers and the distinct outcomes each group will face, emphasizing the importance of obeying the Messenger of Allah (PBUH).

title: The Theme of Surat Al-Ahqaf (الاٴحقاف) (46)
excerpt: Surat Al-Ahqaf concludes the series of seven Surahs that begin with Ha-Mim, all focusing on the Quran as a divine revelation from Allah.

title: The Theme of Surat Al-Jathiyah (الجَاثیَة) (Kneeling) (45)
excerpt: The central theme addresses the two primary reasons for rejecting Allah's message: arrogance and desire-following, along with Allah's response to them.

title: Theme of Surat Fussilat (فُصّلَت) (Expounded) (41)
excerpt: Surat Fussilat, also known as Ha Mim Al-Sajda, is one of the seven surahs beginning with Ha Mim. All revealed in Mecca, they emphasize the clarity of Allah's final message.

title: The Theme of Surat Ad-Dukhan (الدّخان) (Smoke) (44)
excerpt: The central theme revolves around two significant events: the revelation of the Quran and a smoke that will engulf the earth as a sign of the impending end times.

title: The Theme of Surat Az-Zukhruf (الزّخرُف) (Adornments) (43)
excerpt: The main theme is that materialistic distractions, though enticing, are transient and should not divert us from the true path of Allah.

title: The Quranic Definition of Taqwa
excerpt: Exploring the primary sources of knowledge in Islam and how the Quran defines Taqwa — a central concept often misunderstood.

title: The Theme of Surat Ghafir (The Forgiver) (غَافر) (40)
excerpt: Surah Ghafir along with six consecutive Surahs were revealed in Mecca, all beginning with Ha Mim (حٰمٓ), emphasizing the Quran's status as divine revelation.

title: Surat Al-Zumar (الزُّمَر) (The Groups)
excerpt: The main theme is the importance of sincerity (ikhlas) to Allah in all actions. The surah provides ways to assess the level of one's sincerity.

title: Theme of Surat As-Saffat (Devotional Ranks) (37)
excerpt: Surat As-Saffat, revealed in Mecca, emphasizes the importance of submitting to Allah's commands even when the wisdom behind them is unclear to us.

title: Theme of Surat Yaseen (یسٓ) (36)
excerpt: The central theme revolves around the individual duty to convey Allah's message faithfully and without alteration, unlike previous religions where followers compromised divine teachings.

title: Theme of Surat Saba' (Sheba) (سَبَإ) (34)
excerpt: The theme is that preservation of civilization is directly linked to acknowledging and showing gratitude for the blessings bestowed by Allah.

title: Theme of Surat Al-Ahzab (الاٴحزَاب) (The Confederates) (33)
excerpt: The theme is maintaining submission to Allah even in difficult and embarrassing situations. The depth of belief is gauged by one's acceptance of His will.

title: The Theme of Surat Luqman (31)
excerpt: The theme is the importance of wisdom. The best wisdom can be sought from the Quran, the book of wisdom.

title: Surat As-Sajdah (Prostration) (32)
excerpt: The theme is willing submission to Allah. There are two kinds: compulsory submission and voluntary submission. The Prophet (PBUH) often recited this Surat in Friday morning prayer.

title: The Theme of Surat Al-Rum (The Romans) (30)
excerpt: The theme is that the signs of Allah are so clear to demonstrate His lordship and full control over the universe and everything that happens in it.

title: Theme of Surat Al-Ankaboot (The Spider) (29)
excerpt: The theme is that life is a series of trials and success will always be for those who are truthful in their belief in Allah.

title: The Theme of Surah Al-Qasas (28)
excerpt: Its theme is that Allah always prevails, and Allah's promises will always be fulfilled. Demonstrated by focusing on the story of Prophet Musa (موسى).

title: The Theme of Surat Al-Naml (The Ants) (27)
excerpt: The theme is the importance of technological advances in supporting the propagation of Allah's message. Nations that are leaders in science and technology are more effective at conveying the message.

title: The Theme of Surat Al-Shu'ara (The Poets) (26)
excerpt: The theme is the effective methods for inviting people to Islam (Dawah), highlighting the importance of mass media and the art of debate.

title: The Theme of Surah Al-Nur (The Light) (24)
excerpt: The Surat focuses on social manners (الآداب الاجتماعية) that regulate personal life and relationships to ensure harmony and high morality.

title: Surah Al-Mu'minun (المؤمنون) (The Believers) (23)
excerpt: This Surah lists the most important traits of believers, providing a scorecard to judge themselves before they are judged by Allah.

title: The Theme of Surah Al-Hajj (الحج) (The Pilgrimage) (22)
excerpt: The Hajj is a journey that should inspire pilgrims to reaffirm their belief in the Oneness of God and commitment to unity, humility, and social responsibility.

title: The Theme of Surat Al-Anbiya (الاٴنبیَاء) (The Prophets) (21)
excerpt: The theme is the role of the Prophets: to deliver a singular message that there is no god but Allah, so worship Him.

title: The Theme of Surat Ta-Ha (طه) (20)
excerpt: Life becomes easy when following Allah's guidance and becomes difficult when deviating from it. 'We have not sent down the Qur'an unto you to cause you difficulty.'

title: Allah's Name Al-Lateef (اللطيف)
excerpt: The name Al-Lateef is derived from the root 'la-ṭa-fa' (لَطَفَ), meaning being gentle, kind, merciful, subtle, not overbearing and undetectable.

title: The Theme of Surat Maryam (مريم) (Mary) (19)
excerpt: The theme is that the most important strategy to preserve any religion is through having offspring. The Surat links children to the inheritance of religion.

title: Allah's Name Al-Haleem (الحليم) (The Forbearing)
excerpt: The name Haleem is derived from the root h-l-m (حِلم) meaning forbearance, leniency, clemency, gentleness, deliberation, and moderation.

title: The Theme of Surat Al-Kahf (The Cave) (الكهف) (18)
excerpt: The theme is the nature of the challenges and testing that people face to assess the degree of truthfulness of their belief in Allah.

title: Surah Al-Nahl (Bees) (النحل) (16)
excerpt: Called 'The Surat of Blessings' (سورة النعم) because its theme is the enumeration of the blessings of Allah and that He is the provider of all blessings.

title: True Competition: Taqwa and Quran – Khutbah at RICC
excerpt: A post-Ramadan Eid khutba arguing that true competition is in Taqwa, not wealth or status. Uses stories of Abu Bakr vs. Umar in charity, and Al-Hasan ibn Ali's forgiveness.

title: Quran, Taqwa, and Qadr of Allah – Khutbah at RICC
excerpt: Final Jumu'ah of Ramadan. Deep linguistic analysis of Arabic root Q-D-R (ق د ر): Qadar (destiny) vs. Qadr (honor/greatness). Laylat al-Qadr as the night one truly recognizes Allah's greatness.

title: The Pyramid of Faith: From Islam to Ihsan and Taqwa – Khutbah
excerpt: Pre-Ramadan khutba presenting faith as a 10-level pyramid from Muslim to Mu'min to Ihsan. All three categories of believers in Surat Fatir are 'chosen' by Allah — Ramadan is the hospital for the struggling.
